meta.title: Mattingley Manor and Village History
meta.subtitle: Hampshire, England — Anglo-Saxon Period to 1500 AD
meta.primary_source: Victoria County History Hampshire vol. 4, pp. 44-51 (BHO)

etymology.source: Reaney & Wilson, Dictionary of English Surnames, Routledge 1991

domesday_entry.note: Reference i,505a is distinct from Hugh de Port's Heckfield entry at i,483b, suggesting a separate tenant-in-chief

post_conquest_holder_1086.name: Alsi the son of Brixi
post_conquest_holder_1086.overlord_1086: Probable Crown/royal demesne (Holdshot was royal hundred until 1228)
post_conquest_holder_1086.confidence: CONFIRMED for holder; PROBABLE for royal overlordship

lords.name: Matta
lords.notes: Name inferred from place-name only. Old English personal name Matta documented. No independent historical record of this individual.
sources: Place-name etymology

lords.name: Alric
lords.notes: Free landholder, no feudal superior. No further biographical detail available.
sources: Domesday Book 1086
VCH Hampshire vol. 1, p. 505a

lords.name: Alsi the son of Brixi
lords.notes: Domesday tenant 1086. Probably held of the Crown given Holdshot hundred was royal. Relation to Ellis (next holder, 1167) unknown.
sources: Domesday Book 1086
VCH Hampshire vol. 1, p. 505a
VCH vol. 4, p. 47, fn. 78

lords.name: Ellis
lords.notes: Named only as 'Ellis, the owner in 1167.' No surname or patronymic. No documented feudal superior. The VCH says he was 'apparently succeeded by Revelendus' — probable but not stated as direct succession in the primary source.
sources: Pipe Roll 13 Henry II (1167), Pipe Roll Society vol. xi, p. 189 — VCH vol. 4, p. 47, fn. 79

lords.name: Revelendus
lords.landholding: Two knights' fees in Oakley and Mattingley (Rot. de Oblat. et Fin., p. 218)
lords.notes: Succeeded Ellis. By wife Rose left three sons among whom his possessions were divided. The Merton Priory cartulary (Cotton MS Cleopatra C. vii) is the primary source. VCH says Ellis 'apparently' preceded him — succession is PROBABLE not CONFIRMED.
sources: Cotton MS Cleopatra C. vii, fol. 134d — VCH vol. 4, fn. 80

lords.name: James de Oakley
lords.relation: Son of Revelendus, elder brother of Stephen de Mattingley
lords.role: Holder of James's share of Mattingley
lords.notes: Received his share of Revelendus's property. Had a son Hugh who granted his Mattingley property to Merton Priory. James himself probably died without male issue.
sources: Cotton MS Cleopatra C. vii, fol. 134d

lords.name: Bartholomew de Oakley
lords.relation: Son of Revelendus, elder brother of Stephen de Mattingley
lords.role: Holder of Bartholomew's share; transferred to Stephen

child.name: Rose, daughter of Bartholomew de Oakley
child.notes: Paid King John 100 shillings in 1204 for her share of the two knights' fees in Oakley and Mattingley. Did not recover the land.
child.source: Rot. de Oblat. et Fin., p. 218

lords.notes: Gave his inheritance in Mattingley to his brother Stephen de Mattingley. Predeceased 1204. Had a daughter Rose.
sources: Cotton MS Cleopatra C. vii, fol. 135
Rot. de Oblat. et Fin., p. 218 — VCH vol. 4, fn. 82

lords.name: Hugh de Oakley
lords.relation: Son of James de Oakley; grandson of Revelendus
lords.role: Granted his Mattingley property to Merton Priory
lords.notes: Granted his property in Mattingley to the Prior and convent of Merton. Probably died without issue. The Merton grant is documented.
sources: Cotton MS Cleopatra C. vii, fol. 134 — VCH vol. 4, fn. 81

lords.name: Stephen de Mattingley
lords.notes: Youngest son of Revelendus. Received his own share PLUS Bartholomew's share. In 1206 granted to Walter Prior of Merton: 6 virgates, 30 acres, meadows, 6s. 3d. rent, and a mill. Recorded in Curia Regis Rolls as 'Stephen de Madingel', de Matingel''. Succeeded by son Peter.

grant_to_merton_1206.date: Trinity Term, 8th year of King John (1206)
grant_to_merton_1206.grantee: Walter, Prior of Merton, and his successors
items: a mill on the River Whitewater
grant_to_merton_1206.source: Feet of Fines, Hampshire, Trinity 8 John (1206), TNA CP 25/1

sources: Feet of Fines, Hampshire, Trinity 8 John (1206) — TNA CP 25/1; VCH vol. 4, fn. 83
Curia Regis Rolls — cited in Reaney & Wilson, Dict. Eng. Surnames (1991)
Cotton MS Cleopatra C. vii, fol. 134d

lords.name: Peter de Mattingley
lords.notes: Son and successor of Stephen de Mattingley. Sold all his property to Geoffrey de Arundel. Recorded as 'Peter Mattingeley' in Hampshire source dated 1249 (Reaney & Wilson 1991). This is the last documented Mattingley-surname holder of the manor.
sources: Cotton MS Cleopatra C. vii, fol. 134d — VCH vol. 4, fn. 84
Feet of Fines, Hampshire, Michaelmas 21 Hen. III (1236) — VCH vol. 4, fn. 86
Reaney & Wilson, Dictionary of English Surnames (1991) — 'Peter Mattingeley 1249'

lords.name: Geoffrey de Arundel
lords.role: Lord of Mattingley (purchaser from Peter de Mattingley)
lords.notes: Bought all of Peter de Mattingley's property. Sought to recover from Merton Priory the land granted by Hugh de Oakley. In Michaelmas 1236 acknowledged Merton's rights and both he and Emma were received into the prayers of Merton church. Subsequently sold his remaining Mattingley land to Merton, completing the absorption into Holdshot. NOT the Earl of Arundel — likely a cadet branch of d'Aubigny family or separate Norman 'de Arundel' line. Post-Mattingley career undocumented.

merton_agreement_1236.date: Michaelmas, 21st year of Henry III (= Michaelmas 1236)
merton_agreement_1236.content: Geoffrey acknowledged it to be the right of the prior and convent; both Geoffrey and Emma received into benefits and prayers of Merton
merton_agreement_1236.source: Feet of Fines, Hampshire, Michaelmas 21 Hen. III — VCH vol. 4, fn. 86

sources: Cotton MS Cleopatra C. vii, fol. 134d, fol. 136 — VCH vol. 4, fn. 84, 85, 86
Feet of Fines, Hampshire, Michaelmas 21 Hen. III (1236) — VCH vol. 4, fn. 86

post_1249_holdshot_tenants.name: Merton Priory (institution)
post_1249_holdshot_tenants.note: Entire manor absorbed into Holdshot. Merton Priory had held increasing portions from c.1206 (Stephen's grant) and c.1220 (Hugh de Oakley's grant). Geoffrey de Arundel's sale completed the merger c.1236-1250.

post_1249_holdshot_tenants.name: Thomas de Kent
post_1249_holdshot_tenants.role: Prior of Merton — granted Holdshot life tenancy
post_1249_holdshot_tenants.source: Cotton MS Cleopatra C. vii, fol. 62, 62d, 75d

post_1249_holdshot_tenants.name: Sir James de Woodstock
post_1249_holdshot_tenants.role: Life tenant of Holdshot from Merton Priory
post_1249_holdshot_tenants.source: Inq. p.m. 15 Edw. III, 1st nos., no. 18; Close, 15 Edw. III, pt. i, m. 44

post_1249_holdshot_tenants.name: Thomas de Foxley
post_1249_holdshot_tenants.role: Life tenant of Holdshot (via widow Joan)
post_1249_holdshot_tenants.note: Married Joan, widow of Sir James de Woodstock. Separate person from Thomas de Foxley who imparked Bramshill/Hazeley in 1347.

post_1249_holdshot_tenants.name: Rycher family
post_1249_holdshot_tenants.period: Mid-14th century to at least late 15th century
sources: Feet of Fines, Hampshire, Hillary 14 Edw. III (c.1340)
De Banco R. Mich. 13 Hen. VII, m. 359 (c.1497)

hazeley_portion.note: A portion of Hazeley Heath within modern Mattingley parish belonged to the lords of Bramshill.

persons.name: John de Foxley
persons.seised_at_death: 58 acres and 3 roods in Hazeley Heath
sources: Chart. R. 10 Edw. II, m. 12
Inq. p.m. 18 Edw. II, no. 38

persons.name: Thomas de Foxley
persons.note: Obtained licence to impark 2,500 acres in Bramshill and Hazeley
persons.source: Pat. 21 Edw. III, pt. iv, m. 19

holdshot_to_crown.instrument: Mins. Accts. Surr. 36 & 37 Hen. VIII, no. 187, m. 9d

grant_to_paulet.source: Pat. 36 Hen. VIII, pt. xvi, m. 3

bells.description: Smaller bell, no inscription, possibly 13th century

earliest_document.description: Wykeham licence to officiate in chapel at Mattingley

plate.description: Silver chalice and paten cover, both 1568

registers.note: Begin with first year of compulsory registration under Cromwell's 1538 order
